creating-folders-and-files.label: Creating Folders and Files
creating-folders-and-files.summary: Use `mkdir`, `mkdir -p`, and `touch` to prepare useful project folders.

mkdir-creates-folders.title: Folders first, files second
mkdir-creates-folders.bodyMarkdown:
`mkdir` creates one folder. `mkdir -p` creates a whole path if parts are missing. `touch` creates an empty file or updates its timestamp.

```bash
mkdir -p school/biology/week1
touch school/biology/week1/notes.txt
```

mkdir-p-nested.title: Nested folders keep work organized
mkdir-p-nested.bodyMarkdown: A nested folder path tells a story: project, category, then file. The path `website/pages/home.html` tells you exactly what the file is for.

touch-creates-files.title: Check the shape after creating it
touch-creates-files.bodyMarkdown: After creating folders and files, run `ls` on the folder you just changed. Small checks help you catch typos before they spread.
